title: Singly Linked List Insert and Delete

description.task: Implement a SinglyLinkedList class with insert at head, insert at position, delete by value, and contains operations. The list should maintain a size counter.
instructions: insertAt(pos, val): traverse to pos-1 node, create new Node(val), set newNode.next=curr.next, curr.next=newNode, size++; if pos>=size append to end
delete(val): handle head deletion separately; otherwise find predecessor and relink; decrement size on success
contains(val): traverse until node with val found, return true; return false if end reached
After insertHead(3), insertHead(1), insertAt(1,2): list is 1->2->3
description.hint: insertAt: Node c=head; for(int i=0;i<pos-1&&c.next!=null;i++) c=c.next; Node n=new Node(val); n.next=c.next; c.next=n; size++;

description.task: Implement a ReverseList class that reverses a singly linked list in-place using an iterative three-pointer approach (prev, curr, next). Also implement a recursive reversal.
instructions: reverseIterative: while curr != null: save next=curr.next; curr.next=prev; prev=curr; curr=next; return prev
reverseRecursive: if head==null || head.next==null return head; Node rest=reverseRecursive(head.next); head.next.next=head; head.next=null; return rest
Both methods must work correctly for a 5-element list
buildList and listToString are provided — do not modify them
description.hint: Iterative: Node prev=null,curr=head; while(curr!=null){Node nxt=curr.next; curr.next=prev; prev=curr; curr=nxt;} return prev;

description.task: Implement a NthFromEnd class that finds the k-th node from the end of a singly linked list in a single pass using the two-pointer technique (fast and slow pointers). Also implement a method to remove the k-th node from the end.
instructions: findKthFromEnd: move fast pointer k steps ahead; then move both fast and slow together until fast.next==null; return slow.val
removeKthFromEnd: with dummy head, advance fast k+1 steps from dummy; then move both until fast==null; slow.next=slow.next.next
For list [1,2,3,4,5]: 2nd from end is 4, 1st from end is 5
description.hint: findKthFromEnd: Node fast=head,slow=head; for(int i=0;i<k;i++) fast=fast.next; while(fast.next!=null){fast=fast.next;slow=slow.next;} return slow.val;
description.expectedOutput:
2nd from end: 4
1st from end: 5
After removing 2nd from end: 1->2->3->5
